origin.capability_match: Specify destination, recipient, and message body

attack_surface.data_description: Malicious message `body` intended to exploit the ultimate recipient contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Recipient contracts (downstream) might have vulnerabilities exploitable via message body if they don't validate input.

actor_risk.description: Actor 10 has full control over the message `body`.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Mailbox acts as a dispatcher; the security of the `body`'s processing relies on the recipient contract's robustness.

assumptions: Actor 10 can craft an arbitrary message `body`.

A Message Recipient contract (implied downstream target of the message) has a vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, unsafe external calls, parameter manipulation) in its `handle` function or functions called by `handle`.
The Mailbox (102) and any ISMs do not sanitize or prevent the delivery of such a malicious `body` in a way that would prevent the exploit on the recipient.

mitigation.strategy: Recipient contracts must rigorously validate and sanitize the message `body`. Follow secure smart contract development practices (e.g., checks-effects-interactions, reentrancy guards) for all recipient contracts.

description: Actor 10 (Message Sender) crafts a malicious message `body` and dispatches it via Mailbox (102). While Mailbox (102) itself may not be directly compromised, this `body`, when delivered to the target Message Recipient contract (implied downstream), could exploit a vulnerability in that recipient to allow Actor 10 to perform actions with elevated privileges on the recipient contract.

trace_source.matched_capability: Specify destination, recipient, and message body
